heading: Deploy a Sharded Cluster
body:
> **Tip:** 
>
> You can configure [Online Archives](online-archive-overview) to
> move infrequently accessed data from your Atlas cluster to a
> MongoDB-managed read-only federated database instance instead of sharding your collection
> or upgrading your cluster tier. To learn more about Online Archive, see
> [Manage Online Archives](manage-online-archive).

To deploy your cluster as a [sharded cluster](manual/reference/glossary),
toggle **Shard your cluster (M30 and up)** to `Yes`.

[sharded cluster](manual/reference/glossary) support horizontal scaling
and consist of [shards](manual/core/sharded-cluster-shards),
[config servers](manual/core/sharded-cluster-config-servers) and
[mongos](manual/reference/glossary) routers.  To learn more, see
[About Config Servers Deployment](nodes-for-config-server). Config servers must remain readable for sharded read operations to continue to function.

If you enable Atlas-managed config servers, Atlas may colocate config server
data with application data instead of using a dedicated config server.
To learn more, see [Atlas-Managed Config Servers for Sharded Clusters](config-server-management).
